questions.question: What's your go-to phrase for a compliment?

B.text: J'adore ton sourire. (I love your smile.)

D.text: Tu as de beaux yeux. (You have beautiful eyes.)

questions.question: How would you express your desire to be close to them?

C.text: J'ai envie de toi. (I want you.)

D.text: Je souhaite passer tout mon temps avec toi. (I wish to spend all my time with you.)

questions.question: Your partner had a bad day. What do you say?

A.text: Je suis là pour toi. (I'm here for you.)

B.text: Ne t'inquiète pas, ça va aller. (Don't worry, it will be okay.)

C.text: Veux-tu parler ? (Do you want to talk about it?)

D.text: Laisse-moi te faire oublier tout ça. (Let me make you forget all about it.)

questions.question: What's your choice for a playful, flirty comment?

A.text: Tu me rends fou/folle. (You drive me crazy.)

C.text: Arrête de me regarder comme ça... (Stop looking at me like that...)

D.text: Je t'ai cuisiné ton plat préféré. (I cooked your favorite dish.)

questions.question: How would you end a romantic evening?

A.text: Veux-tu rester la nuit ? (Would you like to stay the night?)

B.text: Je ne veux pas que cette soirée se termine. (I don't want this evening to end.)

C.text: J'ai adoré chaque instant passé avec toi. (I loved every moment spent with you.)

D.text: On remet ça quand ? (When are we doing this again?)

The Classic Romantic.description: Your seduction style is timeless and heartfelt, using classic phrases that never fail to touch the heartstrings. Romance is not just in grand gestures for you but in the small, tender moments.

The Passionate Poet.description: You wield words like a beautifully sharp sword, each phrase chosen to ignite passion and desire. For you, romance is a fiery dance, and you're always ready to lead.

The Playful Partner.description: You believe in the power of a playful tease and a hearty laugh. For you, seduction is as much about intellectual play as it is about physical attraction. Your partner is kept on their toes, never quite knowing what fun comes next.

The Sensitive Soul.description: To you, true seduction lies in showing vulnerability and being present. Your approach is genuine and caring, always aiming to create a safe and comforting space for your partner.

The Comforting Ballad.description: Echoing the sentiments of 'Make You Feel My Love' by Adele, you value a supportive and understanding partnership. In your ideal relationship, comfort, and emotional safety come above all, ensuring you and your partner always feel loved.

52040d9337cf9c55c94aa500.title: Discover Your Marriage Myth Preferences
52040d9337cf9c55c94aa500.description: Marriage is surrounded by myths that can shape our perceptions in unique ways. This quiz explores which popular marriage myths you might unconsciously align with or believe. Through a series of questions, discover more about your views on marriage. Choose the answer that resonates most with you to uncover your marriage myth preference.

questions.question: How important do you think communication is in a marriage?

A.text: Essential above all; without it, marriage cannot succeed.

B.text: Important, but actions speak louder than words.

C.text: It's somewhat important, but finding a perfect soulmate matters more.

D.text: Overrated; some things are better left unsaid.

questions.question: What's your take on the idea that 'marriages are made in heaven'?

A.text: Absolutely true; there's a perfect match for everyone.

B.text: Partially true; love at first sight exists, but effort is needed.

C.text: Mythical; successful marriages are built on hard work, not destiny.

D.text: I'm skeptical; I believe more in convenience and practicality.

questions.question: How do you feel about the idea that 'marriage will complete you'?

A.text: Completely agree; my other half is out there.

B.text: Somewhat agree; it's important, but I'm already complete.

C.text: Disagree; I'm whole on my own, a partner is just a bonus.

D.text: Strongly disagree; expecting another to complete you is unhealthy.

questions.question: What do you think about the myth 'Happy couples never fight'?

A.text: It's true; true love means no conflicts.

B.text: Somewhat true; minor disagreements happen, but not fights.

C.text: False; healthy conflicts are part of every strong marriage.

D.text: Completely false; fighting is natural and sometimes necessary.

questions.question: How do you view the myth 'You should always feel in love'?

A.text: Absolutely; love should be constant and overwhelming.

B.text: Mostly true; you should at least always feel some spark.

C.text: Not quite; love evolves and isn’t always felt intensely.

D.text: False; love is a decision, not just a feeling.

The Romantic Idealist.description: You lean towards the more traditional and romanticised myths about marriage. Believing in soulmates and enduring love, you may view marriage through rose-colored glasses.

The Practical Lover.description: You strike a balance between idealism and realism. You believe in love and its power, yet you understand that effort and practicality play a crucial role in a successful marriage.

The Realist Partner.description: You debunk popular marriage myths, focusing on hard work, communication, and realistic expectations. Your approach to marriage is practical, yet you believe deeply in love's enduring value.

The Independent Thinker.description: You challenge conventional marriage notions, emphasizing personal fulfillment and the importance of being complete alone. Your views encourage healthy self-dependence within a partnership.

questions.question: How do you feel about makeup brands that have had controversy over inclusivity and diversity?

B.text: I'm cautious. I prefer brands that learn from their mistakes and improve their practices.

C.text: It doesn't significantly influence my purchasing decision, though I notice it.

D.text: It's not a factor for me as long as the product is good.

questions.question: What's your stance on makeup brands using synthetic and non-biodegradable materials in their products?

A.text: I avoid them entirely and look for sustainable and eco-friendly options.

B.text: I lean towards eco-friendly brands but will make exceptions for certain products.

C.text: It's not my top concern, but it's nice when brands are environmentally conscious.

D.text: I don't consider this factor at all when purchasing makeup.

questions.question: How do you react to brands that are often called out for overly edited and unrealistic advertising?

A.text: I stop supporting them completely. Honesty and transparency are crucial for me.

B.text: I'm wary of such brands, but if their products work for me, it's not a huge issue.

C.text: It doesn't really affect me. All brands do it to some extent.

D.text: I don't care about the advertising as long as I like the products.

questions.question: What's your take on supporting local/small makeup brands?

A.text: I always choose them over big names to support small businesses.

B.text: I prefer them, especially if they align with my ethical standards.

C.text: I'm indifferent. Price and quality matter more to me than the size of the brand.

D.text: I tend to stick with well-known brands for reliability and consistency.

The Ethical Idealist.description: You hold your makeup brands to the highest ethical standards, prioritizing cruelty-free, inclusivity, sustainability, transparency, and community support above all else.

The Conscientious Consumer.description: You strive for balance, appreciating brands that show an effort to improve and align with ethical practices. You make thoughtful choices, considering both quality and values.

The Practical Purchaser.description: For you, the product's quality and effectiveness come first, though you're not indifferent to ethical concerns. You might prefer ethical brands but not at the expense of performance.

The Unconcerned User.description: Your focus is strictly on the product's performance and quality, without much consideration for the ethical implications of the brand's practices. You value personal satisfaction over corporate responsibility.

D.text: Acta Non Verba - Actions, not words. I tackle challenges with action

B.text: Per Aspera Ad Astra - Through hardships to the stars. Setbacks are a path to growth

D.text: Fortes Fortuna Adiuvat - Fortune favors the bold. I take setbacks as opportunities for new adventures

C.text: Amor Vincit Omnia - Love conquers all. I am motivated by love and passion

D.text: Perseverando - By persevering. I stay motivated by never giving up

C.text: Sui Generis - One of a kind. I embrace my uniqueness and navigate change with confidence

D.text: Solvitur Ambulando - It is solved by walking. I find clarity through taking action amidst change

questions.question: How do you approach self-reflection?

A.text: Radix Malorum Est Cupiditas - Greed is the root of all evil. I reflect on my actions and strive for self-improvement

B.text: Qui Tacet Consentire Videtur - Silence gives consent. I reflect through introspection and quiet contemplation

C.text: Ad Astra Per Aspera - To the stars through hardships. I reflect on my journey and learn from past experiences

D.text: Vis Per Scientiam - Strength through knowledge. I reflect by seeking wisdom and understanding

521a12c937cf9c55c94abbd4.title: Discover Your Resilience
521a12c937cf9c55c94abbd4.description: This quiz will help you uncover your resilience and coping mechanisms when dealing with your parents' divorce. Choose the answer that best represents your feelings or behavior in each situation.

questions.question: How do you typically handle conflicts between your parents?

B.text: I withdraw and avoid getting involved.

C.text: I take a side and defend one parent.

D.text: I express my frustration and emotions openly.

questions.question: What do you do when you feel overwhelmed by the situation?

A.text: I talk to a trusted friend or family member.

B.text: I distract myself with activities or hobbies.

C.text: I keep my feelings to myself and pretend everything is fine.

D.text: I seek professional support or counseling.

questions.question: How do you react when people ask about your parents' divorce?

A.text: I provide a simple and honest explanation.

B.text: I avoid talking about it and change the subject.

C.text: I get defensive and protective of my family.

D.text: I feel uncomfortable and prefer not to discuss it.

questions.question: What helps you feel better during tough times?

A.text: Spending time with friends or loved ones.

B.text: Engaging in physical exercise or outdoor activities.

C.text: Engaging in creative outlets like arts or writing.

D.text: Having alone time to reflect and recharge.

A.text: I believe it will lead to personal growth and resilience.

The Resilient Rock.description: You have shown incredible resilience and strength in navigating your parents' divorce. Your ability to handle conflicts, seek support, and maintain a positive outlook is truly admirable.

The Silent Survivor.description: You tend to withdraw and keep your feelings to yourself when dealing with your parents' divorce. While you may be resilient in your own way, opening up and seeking support can greatly enhance your coping mechanisms.

The Loyal Protector.description: You take on the role of protecting and defending your family during the divorce. While loyalty is commendable, it's important to ensure that you also prioritize your own emotional well-being and seek support when needed.

The Emotional Rollercoaster.description: You experience a range of emotions and may find it challenging to cope with your parents' divorce. This is completely normal and seeking professional support or counseling can provide you with the tools to navigate this difficult period.

The Adventurer.description: You like to break away from traditional flavors and try something new. You are open to experimenting with unique combinations and enjoy the thrill of culinary exploration.

The Curious Scholar.description: You are driven by a deep curiosity about how societies and cultures have evolved over time. History is a never-ending source of fascination for you.

questions.question: How do you typically express your emotions to your partner?

A.text: I am open and honest about my feelings.

B.text: I tend to keep my emotions to myself.

C.text: I express my emotions indirectly through actions or body language.

D.text: I often get overwhelmed and don't express my emotions effectively.

questions.question: When you have a disagreement with your partner, how do you usually handle it?

A.text: I prefer calm discussions and finding compromises.

B.text: I avoid confrontation and try to let it go.

C.text: I tend to get defensive and argumentative.

D.text: I often feel attacked and respond with anger or silence.

questions.question: How comfortable are you with expressing your needs and expectations in a relationship?

A.text: I am very comfortable and assertive in expressing my needs.

B.text: I struggle with expressing my needs clearly and directly.

C.text: I often expect my partner to read my mind.

D.text: I find it difficult to identify and communicate my needs.

questions.question: In times of conflict, how do you usually approach finding a solution?

A.text: I am proactive and willing to work towards a resolution.

B.text: I tend to withdraw and wait for the issue to resolve itself.

C.text: I try to control the situation and have things go my way.

D.text: I often feel helpless and unsure about how to resolve conflicts.

questions.question: How do you typically communicate your expectations with your partner?

A.text: I communicate my expectations clearly and directly.

B.text: I assume my partner should know what I expect without communicating it explicitly.

C.text: I drop hints and hope my partner picks up on them.

D.text: I often feel misunderstood and struggle to communicate my expectations.

The Emotionally Open Communicator.description: You are open and honest about your feelings, which helps create a positive and healthy atmosphere in your relationship.

The Conflict Avoider.description: You tend to avoid confrontation and prefer to let issues go, but this may lead to unresolved conflicts.

The Defensive Debater.description: You often get defensive and argumentative during disagreements, which can escalate conflicts in your relationship.

The Insecure Communicator.description: You struggle with expressing your needs and emotions, leading to misunderstandings and frustration.

Tech-savvy Guru.description: You are a true technology enthusiast! You love staying connected and exploring the latest gadgets and digital trends.

Digital Minimalist.description: You prefer to keep things simple and minimize your use of technology. You appreciate the benefits it brings but prioritize offline experiences.

In-between Explorer.description: You have a moderate approach to technology. You enjoy its conveniences but also value face-to-face interactions and unplugged moments.

Tech-phobic Avoider.description: You have a cautious approach to technology and prefer to avoid it whenever possible. You may find it overwhelming or unnecessary in your daily life.

The Expressive Communicator.description: You are comfortable expressing your emotions and opinions openly, and you value direct and honest communication.

The Reserved Communicator.description: You are more reserved in your communication style, allowing others to take the lead and avoiding expressing strong emotions or opinions.

The Movie Marathoner.description: You prefer to spend your winter nights binge-watching movies or TV shows under a warm blanket. You enjoy getting lost in captivating stories and losing track of time.

The Tunnel Vision Victim.description: You struggle to understand your partner's perspective on jealousy and may dismiss their concerns. Try to empathize with their feelings and work together towards creating a more secure and trusting relationship.

561b71e18ebea80ad976f5c1.title: Discover His Interest: Verbal Clues Quiz
561b71e18ebea80ad976f5c1.description: This quiz will help you identify if a guy is interested in you based on the verbal clues he gives. Read each question carefully and choose the option that best matches your experience. At the end, you will receive your result based on your total points.

questions.question: When you talk, does he actively listen and engage in the conversation?

A.text: Yes, he is always attentive and asks follow-up questions.

B.text: Sometimes, he seems interested but can also get distracted.

C.text: Rarely, he often seems disinterested or doesn't pay attention.

questions.question: Does he make an effort to remember the details and small things you tell him?

A.text: Absolutely, he remembers even the smallest details.

B.text: Sometimes, he remembers important things but might forget minor details.

C.text: No, he rarely remembers what I told him.

questions.question: Does he often initiate conversations or reach out to you first?

A.text: Yes, he frequently initiates contact and texts or calls me first.

B.text: Sometimes, he initiates contact but it's not consistent.

C.text: Rarely, I'm usually the one initiating the conversation.

questions.question: Does he compliment or praise you often?

A.text: Yes, he compliments me frequently and appreciates my qualities.

B.text: Sometimes, he compliments me but it's not very frequent.

C.text: Rarely, he rarely compliments me or shows appreciation.

questions.question: Does he use flirty or suggestive language when talking to you?

A.text: Yes, he frequently uses flirty language and makes suggestive comments.

B.text: Sometimes, he occasionally uses flirty language or makes subtle hints.

C.text: No, he rarely uses flirty language or makes any suggestive comments.

The Interested Admirer.description: Based on the verbal clues he gives, it seems like he is genuinely interested in you. He actively listens, remembers details, initiates conversations, compliments you, and uses flirty language. Keep an eye on his actions for more confirmation!

The Maybe-More-Than-Friends.description: He occasionally shows signs of interest, but it's not consistent. He might initiate contact and compliment you sometimes, but there are moments where he seems disinterested. It's hard to tell for sure if he's interested or just being friendly.

The Friendly Acquaintance.description: Based on the verbal clues he gives, it seems like he perceives you as a friendly acquaintance. He rarely initiates contact, doesn't actively listen, rarely compliments, and avoids flirty language. It's unlikely that he has romantic interest.

The Independent Explorer.description: You value your independence and enjoy having your own space and time. You have a healthy level of attachment and are comfortable with both closeness and solitude.

The Comfort-Seeking Companion.description: You thrive on a secure and close bond with your partner. Emotional intimacy is important to you, and you may struggle a bit with being apart for extended periods.

The Insecure Clinger.description: You tend to feel anxious and dependent on your partner. It's important to work on building your self-esteem and finding a healthy balance between independence and closeness.

544805c144835a24a20f3861.title: Sibling Preference Quiz
544805c144835a24a20f3861.description: Discover how you cope with your parents' preference for your sibling and gain insights into your strategies for dealing with this situation.

questions.question: How do you typically respond when your parents show favoritism towards your sibling?

A.text: I try to gain their attention by doing something special or achieving something noteworthy.

B.text: I feel hurt and invisible, and I withdraw emotionally from my parents.

C.text: I confront my parents about the unfairness and express my feelings.

D.text: I try to ignore the situation and focus on my own interests and goals.

questions.question: How do you communicate with your sibling about the preference shown by your parents?

A.text: I avoid discussing the topic and maintain a neutral relationship with my sibling.

B.text: I openly express my frustration and envy towards my sibling.

C.text: I try to support my sibling and maintain a positive relationship, regardless of the preference.

D.text: I try to distance myself from my sibling to avoid further comparisons.

questions.question: How does the preference shown by your parents affect your self-esteem?

A.text: It boosts my determination to prove myself and gain recognition independently.

B.text: It severely damages my self-confidence, and I constantly doubt my worth.

C.text: It doesn't affect my self-esteem much; I believe in my own abilities.

D.text: It motivates me to become better and surpass my sibling in different areas.

questions.question: How do you seek validation in the absence of parental approval?

A.text: I look for validation from other important figures in my life, such as friends or mentors.

B.text: I become self-reliant and focus on achieving personal goals without seeking external validation.

C.text: I struggle with seeking validation and constantly seek approval from my parents.

D.text: I find solace in pursuing activities I enjoy and nurturing my own self-worth.

questions.question: How do you cope with feelings of resentment towards your parents and sibling?

A.text: I express my resentment openly and confront my parents and sibling about their actions.

B.text: I internalize my resentment and harbor bitter feelings towards them.

C.text: I try to let go of resentment and focus on maintaining healthy relationships.

D.text: I distance myself emotionally from my parents and sibling to protect myself.

The Resilient Optimist.description: You have developed robust coping mechanisms and maintain a positive outlook despite parental favoritism. Your self-esteem remains intact, and you seek validation from other sources.

The Emotional Withdrawer.description: You tend to withdraw emotionally when faced with sibling preference, feeling hurt and invisible. It is important to find healthy ways to communicate your feelings and seek support.

The Assertive Communicator.description: You confront your parents about the unfairness and express your emotions openly. Direct communication can be helpful in addressing the situation, but also consider finding ways to build a positive relationship with your sibling.

The Independent Achiever.description: You focus on your own goals and achievements, seeking validation internally. Although self-reliance is valuable, remember the importance of healthy relationships and seeking support when needed.

The Independent Free Spirit.description: You cherish your independence and enjoy the freedom of being single. You are not easily swayed by societal pressure and make decisions that are true to yourself.

The Desperate Settler.description: You feel desperate for commitment and are willing to settle for anyone just to fulfill societal expectations. Remember that true happiness comes from finding the right person, not just settling.

D.text: I don't really care about anniversaries, they're just dates.

results.description: You have high expectations from others and often feel disappointed when they don't meet them. Consider giving people more grace and understanding.

You have low expectations and tend to be understanding even when others fall short. Maintain your positive attitude.
